item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Definitely underrated. Typically these kinds of test logs would end with the D-class committing suicide, going insane or falling to catatonia. This raises interesting questions about the nature of mathematics and reality.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This file is made available under the Creative Commons CC0 1.0 Universal Public Domain Dedication.

Image by Raysonho

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i must admit, that ending caught me off-guard. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I imagined that the foundation wouldn't be too concerned with the subjects gaining intelligence since it only happens in a specific context and the effect itself prevents them from focusing their attention on anything conspicuously dangerous. Using researchers wouldn't make it any easier to cooperate with whoever's using the board either, since it completely takes control of their focus.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I agree with Atlas. Normally SCPs that are tested don't have much known about them. Imagine what would have happened if a researcher was used for testing and disappeared just like the D class.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I disagree. D-class individuals should, under normal circumstances, be able to be easily terminated, so it doesn't seem as if this would be a major issue in most cases.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is great, they made someone do so much math, that they went ahead and calculated a portal to "Mathworld".
I feel like an exploration log for whatever is on the other side would be interesting, but in my opinion, this stands on its own.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm not going to minus vote, but I do have a criticism here. It doesn't make sense to me that they'd test this with D-Class people. There's an obvious danger in allowing dangerous individuals to attain extreme intelligence, even temporarily, and testing this instead with research personal would allow the foundation to attain insights that might further all sorts of research. It just feels natural to me that the Foundation would throw researchers at this thing to get answers to all sorts of stuff and just kind of accept that they're putting those folks into retirement as the tradeoff.

I think if you centered this idea around a conflict like that it becomes much more interesting and might get a better response.

item.title: Re: Junior Staff Post - Info - Open
Junior Staff Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded: Author, I've cleared the tags. If you're not going to add all the tags for your article, it's best to just leave the page untagged and wait for an experienced tagger to take care of it. We have a team of people on the site who tag articles, and they search for pages that have no tags. Adding only a few tags that are required prevents the team from finding the article, so if you're unfamiliar with the tags, it's totally fine to wait for them to take care of it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this scp a lot, the test logs are fun and the execution of the idea was well done. +1
